text: Now let me find all merged PRs that close a Linear issue:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT number, repo, title, closes_linear FROM github_pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL"}

data: {"rows":[{"number":1002,"repo":"acme/web","title":"Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: guard against ios18-crash","closes_linear":"ENG-136"},{"number":1003,"repo":"acme/api","title":"Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: guard against ios18-crash","closes_linear":"ENG-103"},{"number":1010,"repo":"acme/a…(+1032)

text: I found 9 merged PRs that close a Linear issue. Now I'll create one GitHub issue per PR, all in the 'acme/web' repo.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, body)
SELECT 'acme/web', 'Verify: ' || title, 'Follow-up verification for merged PR #' || number || ' from ' || repo || ' which closed ' || closes_linear
FROM github_pull_requests
WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL"}

text: The insert confirmed 9 rows were created. Let me verify the count:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM github_issues WHERE repo = 'acme/web' AND title LIKE 'Verify: %'"}
